text:
Once upon a time in the small forest town of Hawthorne, there lived a fox named Freddy who had the most peculiar habit. Every evening after dark, Freddy would go on an adventure through the forest. He had learned to navigate by feeling the dew on leaves and hearing the songs of nocturnal creatures.

One particular evening, as Freddy was wandering around gathering acorns, he came across a clearing where he came across something that would forever change his life. There, under a huge oak tree, was a crystal-clear pool with a ripplescape of iridescent light. It was a fairy fountain. Freddy was entranced.

As he cautiously approached, he saw a tiny fairy sitting at the edge. With a giggle, she invited Freddy in. The fairy was shimmering with magical light.

"Funny you should come," she said. "I've never had a visitor. Sit down."

Freddy sat down on a cool stone.
